Text: openclaw is overhyped. i built the same thing with claude code + a prompt file + a cron job. took 45 minutes. their waitlist has 12K people for something you can build in an afternoon. am i wrong?
guy in my discord sold 47 notion templates last month for $2.5k. zero ads. just answered questions in reddit threads with a link in his bio
stopped using linktree. put a carrd with email capture instead. went from 0.3% to 4.1% opt-in rate overnight
cursor is mid. claude code in terminal with dangerously-skip-permissions ships faster than any IDE wrapper. fight me in the replies.
one cold email got me a $3.2k retainer. subject line was 8 words. body was 4 sentences. no portfolio link
client asked for a logo. i made it in canva in 11 minutes and charged $400. they loved it. they don't care about your tools
hot take: 90% of "AI wrappers" will be dead by Q4. the ones that survive are the ones that own the data layer, not the model layer. if your moat is "we use GPT-4" you have no moat.
built a gumroad page with 3 products. first one is free. second is $27. third is $97. the free one sells the $97 one for me
found a subreddit with 340k members and zero good resources in the sidebar. made one. now i get 200 clicks a day to my site for free
saas is cooked for solo devs unless you have distribution. the app store gold rush ended 3 years ago. the real money is in boring services sold to boring businesses. plumber SEO > consumer AI app.
the airtable to stripe integration nobody talks about. automate invoicing for service clients. saved me 6 hours a week
stopped writing 2000 word blog posts. started writing 400 word posts with one clear takeaway. traffic up 38% in 3 weeks
everyone building agents should be building MCP servers instead. agents are the car. MCP servers are the gas station. which one has recurring revenue?
my newsletter hit 4.2k subs. sponsorship rate: $150 per issue. that's $600/mo from 1 email a week. math is stupid simple
discovered you can scrape google maps reviews with a free python script and sell local seo audits for $500 each. did 3 this week
confession: i mass-produce PWA apps with claude code overnight loops. 7 apps in 3 weeks. total dev time: maybe 8 hours. the overnight agent did 90% of the work. is this cheating?
pricing trick that works every time. show the monthly cost as daily. "$97/mo" becomes "$3.20 a day" and close rate went from 12% to 31%
launched a paid community at $29/mo. 83 members in 6 weeks. didn't even have a landing page. just a google form and a stripe link
cold email is NOT dead in 2026. what's dead is lazy cold email. "hey {first_name}" is spam. "saw you just launched X on product hunt, here's what your pricing page is missing" is alpha.
best roi on my time this year: 20 minute loom videos replacing sales calls. close rate actually went up. now i do zero calls
ran a flash sale for 72 hours. $19 product normally $47. made $2.8k. just emailed my list twice and posted once on twitter
you have $500 and 30 days to make $5K. pick one: a) cold email service businesses b) flip apps on flippa c) freelance on fiverr/upwork d) build a micro-saas. wrong answers only in the replies.
what's the most underrated tool in your stack right now? mine is visualping. i monitor 200+ competitor pages and get alerts the second anything changes.
honest question: how many of you have actually made money from AI and how many are just building projects that "could" make money? reply with your actual number. $0 is fine. no judgment.

rank these by actual revenue potential (not hype): AI agent consulting, streamer clipping service, MCP server marketplace, faceless youtube channels, niche twitter accounts. i'll post my ranking tomorrow based on real data.
the accounts making real money on X right now aren't the ones with 100K followers. they're the ones with 2K followers, a niche, and a gumroad link in bio. i tracked 50 accounts. the data is wild.

just ran kelly criterion analysis on all my revenue streams. cold outbound came back at "AGGRESSIVE" allocation signal. 87% margins. this is the play. everything else is a distraction until this is at $5K/mo.

there's a free API that lets you pull real-time pricing data from any ecommerce site. i've been using it to find 40%+ margin products for resale. not sharing the name publicly. DM me "arb" if you want the setup.
shipped a new product page today. it's ugly. it's live. it'll make money before i make it pretty. perfectionism is just procrastination in a nice outfit
revenue breakdown feb 2026: templates $1.4k, retainers $3.2k, affiliate $840, community $2.4k. total: $7.8k. no office. no employees. just wifi
lost my biggest client last week. $1.5k/mo gone overnight. woke up this morning and already replaced it with 2 smaller ones. diversify or die
just recorded 14 loom videos in one sitting. batch content creation is brutal but now i don't have to think about sales for 2 weeks
6 months ago i had 340 twitter followers and zero revenue. today: 11.2k followers and $7.8k/mo. the only thing that changed was i started posting daily
found that 68% of my sales come from 3 tweets i posted in january. pinned a cta in my bio. rest is just vibes and trust building
moved my checkout from a separate page to an embedded form on the sales page. conversion rate jumped from 2.1% to 5.7%
